content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string SSS of size NNN. A string is called **good** if


The first character of the string is 000.
The last character of the string is 111.
Count the number of sub-strings in SSS which are **good**. Since the answer might be a large number, print it modulo 109+710^{9} + 7109+7.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains a single string SSS.


Output Format
For each testcase, print the count of good sub-strings modulo 109+710^{9} + 7109+7.
constraints: Each character of string SSS is either 000 or 111.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1:
Following substrings are good :

10010
10010

Test case 2:
Following substrings are good :

010010
010010
010010
010010
